LUMAS CONSULTATION

SIZE GUIDE

Size is more than scale. It’s presence.

The right size changes how a room feels. Here’s how to choose a format that sits right — for your wall, your space, and the piece you love.

Chiron Duong, Portrait no. 216

ROOM BY ROOM

Where the work will live

How to size a piece for every wall — shown alongside the editions selling best in Germany right now.

ABOVE THE SOFA

120–160 cm wide

A balanced focal point that anchors the seating without crowding it.

TIPAim for roughly two-thirds the width of your sofa, hung 15–20 cm above the backrest.

Landscape editions

Bestselling wide works in Germany, this quarter.

ABOVE THE BED

100–140 cm wide

Calm and grounding — it ties the headboard to the room.

TIPCentre it over the bed and keep the height under 100 cm so it stays soft.

GALLERY WALL / NICHE

40–80 cm wide

Intimate and detailed — right alone in a small space, or as part of a cluster.

TIPChoose one focal piece and balance it with smaller works around it.

ENTRYWAY / HALLWAY

70–120 cm tall (portrait)

A vertical format lengthens and lifts a narrow space.

TIPPair it with a console or bench and leave about 20 cm of breathing room on each side.

ABOVE A CONSOLE / SIDEBOARD

80–120 cm wide

Creates rhythm alongside vases, mirrors, or a sculpture.

TIPLeave at least 20 cm between the top of the furniture and the frame.

VISUALISATION SERVICE

See it on your wall, first.

Not sure how a piece will feel in your space? Send us a photo of your room and our team places the artwork exactly where it would hang — then recommends the size and framing that suit your wall.

  2. 2We place the artwork, to scale, where it will hang.
